sprint: Screen 2 Carousel -- Story-Type Worlds' Own Layout/Layer-Pack Content Now Resolves Too
task: Direct, same-day follow-up: a real story-type World with a Scene-converged Layout/Layer Pack (Background fill + a decoration shape) rendered as a plain blank panel in the just-shipped live-render carousel instead of its real content. Root cause: _layoutTheme(s) -- a separate resolution chain from _theme(s)/_artworkTheme(s), used for all Layout/Holder/Place/Layer-Pack resolution -- only ever checked s.artworkTheme or the GLOBAL active theme, never s.theme, so a story-type preview slide (which correctly sets artworkTheme=null) always fell through to the global fallback, bypassing the World's own layouts/layerPack entirely. Fixed with one new, narrowly-scoped field: _layoutTheme now checks s.layoutTheme first (distinct from s.theme/s.artworkTheme, which are already used for other purposes), set only by the carousel's own render helper -- a no-op for every real page in the app, which never sets this field. Verified via a git-stash A/B test reproducing the exact reported symptom (wrong canvas aspect, wrong colours, blank decoration area) before the fix and correct rendering after, plus a new Playwright test using a synthetic story-type theme shaped like a real Scene-converged package. Full regression across the existing 28-script suite and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors.
